First tagged release of **Atrium** — a terminal command center for orchestrating multiple AI coding agents in parallel, each isolated in its own git worktree and tmux session.

Atrium is a divergent fork of [smtg-ai/claude-squad](https://github.com/smtg-ai/claude-squad) (AGPL-3.0); see `NOTICE` for attribution. This release marks the rebrand and the start of independent development.

## Highlights

- Rebranded to `github.com/ZviBaratz/atrium`; binary `atrium` (with `atr` alias).
- Live-state safety: existing `~/.claude-squad` installs are detected and kept on the legacy data dir + tmux socket; fresh installs use `~/.atrium`. Nothing is moved in place.
- Hardened tests: every test package sandboxes `HOME` so `go test ./...` cannot touch live state.
- Reproducible builds: GoReleaser with `-trimpath`, ldflags-injected version, commit-timestamp builds, and per-archive SBOMs (syft).
- Modern CI: golangci-lint v2, CodeQL, Dependabot, split build/lint pipelines — all in report-don't-block mode.
- `justfile` runs every development task; see `just --list`.

## Install

```bash
go install github.com/ZviBaratz/atrium@v0.1.0
```

Or via the install script:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ZviBaratz/atrium/main/install.sh | bash
```

## Known issues

- `TestSessionDeathStopsProbing` is environmentally flaky (real-tmux PTY integration test); skipped in CI.

This is a 0.x release — APIs and behavior may change.
